name: Ray Copeland
abstract: Ray Copeland (July 17, 1926 – May 18, 1984) was an American jazz trumpet player and teacher.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Randy Weston Sextet
Clark Terry and His Big Band

works.t: Blues All Day, Blues All Night

concerts.label: Hubert Laws Afro-Classic Ensemble/ Randy Weston Quintet

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I had the same group. I had Linc Milliman with Jim Kapus. Ray Copeland. ... I had Ray Copeland. He played all these school dates.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I heard the trumpetist Ray Copeland and Jerome Richardson, the reed man, for the first time. They were both fine musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: There was a very fine trumpet player named Ray Copeland. James Cleveland was in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: we did that month with Ray Copelan, GG and Art Blakie, and Coleman Hawkins and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Ray Copeland. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, I remember that session.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: He had his own style. He used Ray Copeland a lot on trumpet...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
